brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +42.0% since Feb 27 — hit $119 intraday on Gulf energy strikes; IEA reserves absorb shock
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Hit $119.11 intraday Mar 19 on IRGC Gulf energy strikes, pulled back on IEA reserve drawdown. Hormuz effectively closed. 3,200 vessels stranded. Source: ICE Futures Europe / CNBC / Bloomberg.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +45.1% since Feb 27 — approached $100 intraday; 14.5-16.5 mbd shortfall persists
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Approached $100 intraday. Tracking Brent as Hormuz shortfall of 14.5-16.5 mbd persists. Combined bypass capacity only 3.5-5.5 mbd. Source: NYMEX / CNBC.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$0.88 / +29.5% since Feb 27 — 8 states above $4, 3 above $5 — diesel $5.07
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Eight states above $4, three above $5. Diesel $5.07/gal. Jones Act waived Mar 18. EIA revised 2026 avg to $3.34. Source: AAA / EIA / CNN.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -261 pts / -3.8% — 2026 closing low; Dow -768 pts; VIX 25.09
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Lowest closing level of 2026 amid energy war escalation and Fed hawkish hold. VIX at 25.09. Source: NYSE / CNBC / FRED.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: 'Largest strike package yet' — Pentagon requests $200B+ supplemental
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 20: 'largest strike package yet' per Hegseth (~20% larger than Day 19). Pentagon requesting $200B+ supplemental. Source: Pentagon briefing / CSIS / Penn Wharton.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (20 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + $200B supplemental requested
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 20. Does not include VA costs or economic impact ($50-210B per Penn Wharton). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: Hegseth: 7,000+ US — 120 vessels, 44 minelayers destroyed — ~21,000+ combined est.
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. Hegseth Mar 19: 7,000+ US targets to date. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~21,000+ at 1,000+/day. 120 Iranian vessels and 44 minelayers destroyed. Source: CENTCOM / Hegseth briefing Mar 19 / Joint Chiefs Caine.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 KIA confirmed + 1 non-hostile death in Kuwait
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 6 at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait (Day 2). 1 died of wounds at Prince Sultan AB, Saudi Arabia (Day 9). 6 killed in KC-135 crash over Iraq (Day 13). 1 non-hostile death in Kuwait. Source: DoD / CENTCOM.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded across all theaters since Feb 28. ~200 total, 180+ returned to duty. Includes casualties at Port Shuaiba, Prince Sultan AB, mine countermeasures, and base attacks. Source: DoD / CENTCOM.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (Hengaw est.)
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties. Health Ministry (as of Mar 19): 1,444 killed, 18,551 injured. Hengaw (5th report, Mar 18): 5,300 killed — 511 civilians (120 minors, 160 women), 4,800+ military. HRANA: 3,114 deaths. Strikes in 178 cities across 25 provinces. Source: Iranian Health Ministry / Hengaw / HRANA / ACLED.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 968+ killed, 2,432+ wounded — 1M+ displaced — widest evacuation since 2006
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and IDF ground operations in southern Lebanon. 968+ killed, 2,432+ wounded, 1,000,000+ displaced. Hezbollah fired 100+ rockets in single barrage Mar 18 — first to reach southern Israel. Widest IDF evacuation order since 2006. Source: Lebanese Health Ministry / OCHA / ACLED.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: Iran: up to 3.2M + Lebanon: 1M+ — internet at 4% — UNHCR / OCHA
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Iran: 600,000-1M households (up to 3.2M people). Lebanon: 1M+. Internet at 4% of normal. 25+ hospitals damaged, 9 forced out of service. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / NetBlocks.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures. IMO 36th Extraordinary Session: 3,200 vessels, 20,000-40,000 seafarers stranded. 22-23 commercial vessel attacks total. Jones Act waived. Source: IATA / Flightradar24 / IMO.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. ~150 at Minab girls' school (Day 1), 120+ minors documented by Hengaw across Iran, 102+ in Lebanon, 2+ in Tehran, 1 in Kuwait. Source: Hengaw / Lebanese Health Ministry / OHCHR.
